records.rationale: The persistent dead-code, complexity, and type-escape assessments do not describe the transform's incorrect scope model. Proving this defect requires transform-specific output or regression-test evidence; a generic switch-scope rule would accuse ordinary correct code.

Rekon now emits semantic.cleanupCompleteness from source-matched fail-fast lifecycle evidence in all three affected shutdown-hook implementations. Independent judgment retains every buggy path, while the all-settled and guarded fixed implementations remain clean.

Rekon now emits semantic.dependencyResolution from current source-matched evidence on the buggy revision. The candidate identifies the later-match overwrite path, remains a semantic claim pending independent judgment, and is silent after the first-match fix.

The persistent debt and complexity assessments do not identify retained request objects. A generic property-assignment rule would be noisy; runtime heap, lifecycle, or ownership evidence is needed to prove retention beyond response completion.
Rekon now emits events.inverseListenerDelegation only on the buggy revision. The assessment identifies the off-to-on contract mismatch directly and remains silent after the upstream fix.

Rekon now emits semantic.cacheIntegrity from current source-matched evidence on the buggy compiled-code read/write path. The upstream defect is absent from fix-related lines after integrity verification is added; a separate JSON sidecar candidate remains an independent assessment rather than being hidden by the benchmark.

Rekon now emits semantic.optionPropagation from source-matched callback-backed overrides informed by structured spread, override, fallback, and callback context. Independent judgment retains both buggy publish paths as verification-required when imported contracts remain external, while both fixed nullish-fallback paths remain clean.

Rekon now emits validation.partialAllowlistMatch only on the buggy revision. The assessment is constrained to a named allowlist used in an explicit whole-value rejection branch and remains silent once the regex is anchored.

Rekon now emits semantic.errorPropagation from structured throw guards and error identities plus source-matched downstream handling. Independent judgment retains the merged condition/abort identity on the buggy revision, while the fixed revision with separate ConditionError and AbortError branches remains clean.
